source_jobs.description:
<p><strong>What is Cobre, and what do we do?</strong><br>Cobre is Latin America’s leading instant b2b payments platform. We solve the region’s&nbsp;most complex money movement challenges by building advanced financial<br>infrastructure that enables companies to move money faster, safer, and more&nbsp;efficiently.<br>We enable instant business payments—local or international, direct or via API—all&nbsp;from a single platform.<br>Built for fintechs, PSPs, banks, and finance teams that demand speed, control, and&nbsp;efficiency. From real-time payments to automated treasury, we turn complex financial&nbsp;processes into simple experiences.<br>Cobre is the first platform in Colombia to enable companies to pay both banked and&nbsp;unbanked beneficiaries within the same payment cycle and through a single interface.<br><strong>We are building the enterprise payments infrastructure of Latin America!</strong><br><strong>What we are looking for:</strong><br>The Cobre Infrastructure team and their SRE engineers are professionals who face the&nbsp;daily challenges that allow us to improve the technological level of our products. We enjoy<br>each project or task, giving 100% and learning from each other. Our main goal is to&nbsp;maintain the reliability of our systems. To achieve this, we collaborate with other teams to&nbsp;find the most effective solutions, maintain high-reliability processes, adopt the necessary&nbsp;safety measures and optimize time and cost in every decision.<br><strong>What would you be doing:</strong><br>● Support teams in defining the infrastructure that will support the solution&nbsp;architecture.<br>● Support all the infrastructure (aws services and k8s clusters) and company&nbsp;products Culture zero-downtime deployments.<br>● Assisting with troubleshooting application issues and incidents related with&nbsp;infrastructure services.<br>● Review code instrumentation with development teams and ensure necessary&nbsp;dashboards are created to monitor.<br>● Document and maintain runbooks and procedures, automate as much as possible&nbsp;(AI-driven auto-remediation for incidents) to reduce MTTR.<br>● Perform periodic load and scalability testing to establish baselines, drift, and&nbsp;capacity planning.<br>● Design and implement peak readiness reviews for anticipated high-volume times.<br>● Lead weekly operational state reviews covering performance trends, anomalies,&nbsp;errors and other availability events with SREs, product owners, and development&nbsp;teams.<br>● Contribute to incident management, postmortems, and reliability reviews.<br>● Socialize SRE culture across teams within the organization to publicize the value of&nbsp;SRE, mentor and train other engineers around proactive reliability decision making&nbsp;and planning.</p>
<p><strong>What do you need:</strong><br>● Proven experience of at least 5 years as SRE or DevOps, with a strong focus on&nbsp;highly available and scalable environments, cloud infrastructure, observability, and&nbsp;incident management.<br>● In-depth technical knowledge of microservices architecture and cloud platforms&nbsp;(e.g., AWS, Kubernetes), along with proficiency in Infrastructure as Code (IaC)&nbsp;tools (e.g., Terraform, Pulumi).<br>● Strong mindset for automation and continuous improvement with a huge interest in&nbsp;AIOps / AI-driven auto-remediation (n8n, aws bedrock, python scripting …)<br>● Understanding of secure-by-design infrastructure principles.<br>● Exposure to GitOps and declarative configuration patterns.<br>● Basic knowledge in Port.io or any Internal Developer Platform (Backstage, Cortex).<br>● Strong understanding of monitorin

notes: Cobre is a fintech company with a strong focus on payments and treasury management. The job postings suggest a mix of technical and non-technical roles, with a slight emphasis on operations and finance. The company appears to be growing rapidly, with a high volume of job postings in a short period.
